reasoning: The root cause was identified as significant packet loss on the F1-u interface (Downlink). While volumetric metrics like latency and throughput were ranked high, the Metric Severity Prioritization heuristic was used to prioritize hard errors (packet loss) over volumetric fluctuations. This caused a cascade of degradation across the RAN hierarchy, affecting O-CU, O-DU, and both RICs via the E2 and A1 interfaces.
actionable_steps: 1. Perform a physical layer audit of the F1-u link between the O-CU and O-DU to check for faulty cabling or hardware errors. 2. Verify the MTU (Maximum Transmission Unit) settings on the F1-u interface to ensure there is no packet fragmentation causing to loss. 3. Inspect the transport network for congestion or policing policies that may be dropping downlink packets.
